The first phase needs ten man-hours.
Phase two requires twenty man-hours.
Phase three takes forty man-hours.
Phase four needs eighty man-hours.
Phase five uses one hundred sixty man-hours.
These sum to three hundred ten man-hours.
Time for each phase grows by half.
Total weeks become thirty now.
The plan shifts but order stays same.
All teams must adapt to new pace.
Resources were set for old schedule.
Now work stretches across more time.
Delays hit every single step hard.
We face a tough test of skill.
Fortune favors those who stay calm.
No dot marks end of this line.
Each word counts in this strict text.
